long_description: At [2025-12-09 22:37] UTC IBM Cloud engineers have been alerted by team, to an issue with VSI provisions in the FRA Customers attempting to provision VSIs in FRA with VPN may experience failure with redhat images
close_notes:
Summary(All Timestamps must be in UTC)
Issue Summary: VPN reporting provision failures
SME Findings: sme-team: CSRE is-ng-compute-sre
SME Findings
Reason of Escalation: Red hat vsi failing to provision
Resolution Steps: Provisioning failing with RedHat image. During the activation window of maintenance , provisioning of new Red Hat VSI may have failed. Connectivity for existing gateways was not affected, and existing services operated normally throughout the period.
The issue has been mitigated, and successful provisioning is expected.
Signature of Issue: Provisioning failing with RedHat image. 
Jira/Github/Other Tickets:
Category/Subcategory:
Timeline Summary:
2025-12-09 22:37:00 - Core team received notification in #ipops-production about provisioning issue in eu-de
2025-12-09 22:45:00 - CORE team checked provisioning dashboard and provisioning alerts for EU-DE
2025-12-09 22:51:00 - CORE team started teams call with VPN team 
2025-12-09 22:55:00 - CORE team were informed by VPN team that provisioning with RedHat images are failing
2025-12-09 22:57:00 - warroom-vpc-inc10515600 created for issue.
2025-12-09 23:00:00 - CORE team observed smoke test failures for VPN.
2025-12-09 23:05:00 - INC is upgraded to pCIE as there is a Red Hat maintenance update in channel
2025-12-09 23:08:00 - CORE team observed capsules server showing unhealthy in FRA 
2025-12-09 23:10:00 - CORE team asked Red Hat team implementing maintenance to join call
2025-12-09 23:10:00 - PDE7717472is-ng-compute-sre for CSRE team to join
2025-12-09 23:15:00 - RHEL jenkins job disabled as per update from team
2025-12-09 23:23:00 - Red Hat team informed upgarde is completed and asked if we are still see issues
2025-12-09 23:23:00 - Test provisioning are going correctly now after maintenance.
2025-12-09 23:30:00 - As per Red Hat team notification was sent to customers for maintenance but the failures were not expected so as Discussion with AVM and service team the CIE is set as sev4 and resolved as provisioning is going through now.
Resolution Runbook: Not Needed / Not Available / URL
Caused by Change/Incident: CHG14145121
Warroom: #warroom-vpc-inc10515600
Meeting URL: https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/19%3ameeting_OTFlZGM5ZjctMzRiNy00ZjY3LWEyMzMtYTI5ODhiNTcwNmUw%40thread.v2/0?context=%7b%22Tid%22%3a%22fcf67057-50c9-4ad4-98f3-ffca64add9e9%22%2c%22Oid%22%3a%22ac154ec5-6902-4b7a-b6cb-db52003a1403%22%7d
Outage Start: 2025-12-09 21:54:00
Outage End: 2025-12-09 23:22:00
Detection Time: 2025-12-09 22:22:00
Identification Time:2025-12-09 23:17:00
Mitigation Time: 2025-12-09 23:22:00
CIE pilot/co-pilot/squad names: Saujan/Justin/Akash Sane
CORE Manager names: Joby
Executives(VPs) names:
Team mitigated the CIE: Red Hat Satellite Infrastructure Team
Next Update Time:
current_status_next_steps: The issue has been mitigated, and successful provisioning is expected.
customer_facing_impact:
What customers may experience:
Intermittent VPN connectivity issues when connecting to workloads hosted in Frankfurt.
Potential delays in provisioning Red Hat-based VSIs in Frankfurt.
Services depending on impacted VSIs may show degraded performance or time-outs.
chronology:
Response Team Participants (add abbrev. role and name as needed)
=========================================================
NRE(s):
SRE(s): Jesse Driver,Justin McCright,SAUJAN BOHARA
IKS(s): 
SCE:YUSUF MOHIUDDIN
UPT:Eddie Chen 
CORE: Akash Sane,JOBY GEORGE
AVM(s): Evelyn Artavia Rojas


Slack Channel Details:  
warroom-vpc-inc10515600 https://ibm-cloudplatform.slack.com/archives/C0A2LFRMLF4/p1765321562904969

Teams Mtg Details:  
https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/19%3ameeting_OTFlZGM5ZjctMzRiNy00ZjY3LWEyMzMtYTI5ODhiNTcwNmUw%40thread.v2/0?context=%7b%22Tid%22%3a%22fcf67057-50c9-4ad4-98f3-ffca64add9e9%22%2c%22Oid%22%3a%22ac154ec5-6902-4b7a-b6cb-db52003a1403%22%7d

23:27 Services have recovered and are currently stable. However, the root cause has not yet been confirmed, so recurrence remains possible. The service team is actively monitoring the situation to ensure continued stability.
23:29 Maintenance activities have been completed, and services have recovered. All three satellites are up and healthy, and manual processes are currently running successfully.
